w20: Accelerator-optimized instance featuring one NVIDIA A100 GPU and 85GB RAM for specialized machine learning and AI inference workloads.
c150: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-1g: Accelerator-optimized instance featuring 1 NVIDIA Tesla A100 GPU, 12 vCPUs, and 85GB RAM for specialized compute tasks.
c200: Explore the Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-1g, an accelerator-optimized server with 1 NVIDIA Tesla A100 GPU, 12 vCPUs, and 85GB RAM. Engineered for high-performance AI and machine learning workloads.
w150: The Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized server instance designed for compute-intensive tasks. It utilizes an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon processor, providing 12 vCPUs across 6 physical cores with a 2.2 GHz clock speed. The system includes 85 GB of RAM and features a single NVIDIA Tesla A100 GPU from the Ampere family, equipped with 40 GB of VRAM. No local storage is bundled with this configuration. Benchmark data indicates average performance across general CPU and memory metrics, while demonstrating top-tier results in small and medium-scale large language model inference tasks. The instance supports AVX and AVX512F instruction sets for enhanced computational efficiency. Given the hardware profile and specialized GPU acceleration, this server is suitable for machine learning model training, AI inference, and complex data science jobs requiring dedicated hardware acceleration rather than high-throughput general-purpose processing.
